State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

June 17, 1977

Sherrie Marske
Staff Services Analyst
Department of Youth Authority
4241 Williamsborough Drive,
Suite 115
Sacramento, California 95823

Re: Request for Approval of Non-
Substantive Amendments to the
Conflict of Interest Code for
the Department of Youth Authority

Dear Mrs. Marske:

Attached please find the Conflict of Interest Division's response to your request for approval of non-substantive amendments to your Code. Part II of the memorandum contains staff's recommendations pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18752 et seq.

The items deemed permissible shall take effect on an interim basis from the date of receipt of this letter, pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18752(b), and must be submitted to the Fair Political Practices Commission for permanent approval by January 31 of the year succeeding the calendar year in which the non-substantive alteration is approved.

The non-permissible items must be submitted for approval by the regular amendment process pursuant to Government Code §87306, Amendments for Changed Circumstances.

Sincerely,

Alfredo Flores
Research Specialist
Conflicts of Interest Division
(916)322-6444

AF:ma
Attachment

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE ADMINISTRATION

State of California

Memorandum

To : Sherrie Marsku                                    Date : June 17, 1977
     Department of Youth Authority


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION - Alfredo Flores
        Conflicts of Interest Division

Subject: Youth Authority's Request for Approval of a Non-Substantive
         Amendment to its Conflict of Interest Code Pursuant to
         2 Cal. Adm. Code §18752


I.  The Department of Youth Authority, pursuant to 2 Cal.
    Adm. Code §18752(c), hereby submits for approval the
    following amendments to its Conflict of Interest Code.

    A.  The amendments sought are the result of changed
        circumstances within the agency caused by internal
        reorganization of the agency, resulting in the
        following changes (see attachment).

        1.  The Parole and Institutions Branch has been
            split into two branches and renamed - Institutions and Campus Branch and Parole Services
            Branch, respectively.

            a.  Consequently, the Deputy Director of the
                Institutions and Campus Branch no longer
                has responsibility for services to parolees
                [Category I(C)(1)]. Accordingly, this cate-
                gory is changed to reflect this loss.

                (1) Deputy Director, Campus and Institutions
                    Branch, is charged to I, IIA(1) [previously I, IIA(1), C(1)].

        2.  The creation of the new Parole Services Branch
            results in the creation of two new positions:

            a.  Deputy Director of Parole Services, who is
                also made a member of the Director's Executive Staff. This new position inherits the
                paramilitary category I, IIA(1), C(1).

-2-

D.     Assistant Deputy Director of Parole Services
       is in a newly-created position and is
       also designated I, IIA(1), C(1).

3.     Chiefs of the Northern and Southern Divisions,
       Institutions and Camps Branches, are no longer
       responsible for services to parolees and no
       longer make or participate in decisions affect-
       ing real property.

       a.     Chiefs of the Northern and Southern Division
              disclosure category is therefore changed to
              IIA(1) [previously I, IIA(1), C(1)].

4.     Parole Zone Administrators are renamed Parole
       Regional Administrators, and their duties are
       increased to involve real property decisions.
       Their categories are thus changed to I, IIA(2),
       C(2), from a previous IIA(2) - C(2).

5.     All Parole Zones are changed to Parole Regions.

6.     The following programs have been removed from
       the Chief of the Northern Division Institutions
       and Camps Branch Northern Division and trans-
       ferred to Region 1 and placed under the direc-
       tion of the Regional Administrator of Region 1.

       a.     Pesult - the Regional Administrator's
              categories are thus changed from IIA(2),
              C(2), I, IIA(1), C(2).

              (1)  Project Director - Tri County; Assis-
                   tant Project Director, Tri County are
                   transferred to the Regional Adminis-
                   tration Region 1; and

              (2)  Project Director, Citizens Initiative,
                   is transferred to the Deputy Director
                   of the Parole Services Branch (Branch
                   1).

7.     The following programs have been removed from
       the Chief of the Southern Division Institutions
       and Camps Branch and transferred to Region 3
       under the supervision of the Regional Adminis-
       trator of Region 3.

- 3 -

a. Project Director, Space, and
b. Assistant Project Director, Space.

8. The Divisions of Planning and Program Development and the Division of Program Evaluation
are consolidated to form one Division - the
Division of Planning and Program Analysis,
resulting in the elimination of a designated
position, which is the chief of the Division
of Program Evaluation.

9. Under the Division of Staff Services, Institutions and Camps Branch, the following designated
positions are eliminated: Chief, Operations
Planning Program Monitoring Section, Supervisor
of Correctional Education Program and Community
Control Program Administrator.

10. Finally, in the Northern Division of Institutions
and Camps, under the Baldwin School, the
categories for Superintendent and Assistant
Superintendent should be changed from IIA(2)
to IIA(1), H. The "H" was inadvertently left
off and should be added accordingly.

II. Amendment Classification Permission

The staff's determinations are numerically sequenced
to conform to the amendments listed above.

1. Item #1, containing the Deputy Director of
Institutions and Camps Branch, appears to be
governed by §175.3(b)(1), which provides:

"(i) Non-substantive alterations of
conflict of interest cases shall be limited
to the following:

(1) The reallocations or
reclassifications of previously designated
positions, provided no designated
position are created or deleted,
and provided no overtime disclosure
requirements are modified..."

It would thus appear that Proposed existing
disclosure responsibilities are being modified,
resulting in a deletion of disclosure responsi-
bilities. This can not be regarded as a non-
substantive change under §18752(d) and is therefore
not permissible.

2. Item #2, concerning the creation of two new posi-
tions, Deputy Director of Parole Services and the
Assistant Deputy Director, appears to fall within
§18752(d)(4) which provides:

"(i) The creation of new positions
which must be designated pursuant to
Government Code Section 3730(a), which
are subject to an existing agency disclosure
category restriction or the transfer
of a position previously designated to
a new inclusive category; provided, how-
ever, that any person occupying a position
designated pursuant to this subsection may
within 10 working days of notification of
designation file objection with the filing
office of the agency. The filing of an
objection shall render the proposed desig-
nation ineffective until the provisions of
Government Code Section 3711 are satisfied
with respect to the designation of the
positions."

Under §18752(d), the above-mentioned positions
are to be filled with which must be designated
to Government Code §3730(a), and the new positions
are subject to an existing disclosure category.
Until the Deputy Director of Parole Services and the
Assistant Deputy Director's disclosure categories
are identical to an existing category [I, IIA(1),
IIB], and, if therefore permissible, subject
to the possibility that affected employees may
object pursuant to §18752(d)(4).

3. Item #3, covering the Units of the Northern and
Southern Divisions, Institutions and Camps Branches,
falls within the proviso to §18752(d)(1), and therefore
cannot be non-substantive again, because existing
disclosure categories are modified and is therefore
not permissible.

4. Item #4, concerning the Parole Regional Administrators,
falls within §187.2(d)(4), specifically "...the transfer
of a position previously designated to a more inclusive
category." This change is therefore permissible
however, the Agency must permit the employee to object
within 10 working days, necessitating a full hearing.

5. Item #5, the changing of Parole Zones to Parole Regions
falls within §18752(d)(3), which provides:

"(3) The modification of any provision
of a conflict of interest code, provided no
disclosure or disqualification obligation of
any designated employee is disturbed thereby;..."

This change is permissible.

6. Items #'s 6 and 7, concerning the Chiefs of the Southern
and Northern Divisions' Institutions and Camps Branches,
are previously discussed in J., above.

7. Item #'s 6., and 7., concerning the transfer of
Senior Director - Multi-County Assistant Project Director -
Tri-County Project Director - Citizen's Initiative,
Project Director - Space and Assistant Project Director -
Space, all within §18752(d)(1) and is permissible.

8. Item #8, concerning the consolidation of the Divisions
of Planning and Program Development and the Division of
Program Evaluation and the elimination of the
Chief of the Division of Program Evaluation, a previously
designated position, falls within §18752(d)(1), and is,
therefore, not permissible because a position is deleted.

9. Item #9, dealing with the elimination of various desig-
nated positions under the Division of Staff Services
Institutions and Camps Branch, falls within the proviso
to §187.2(d)(4) and is, therefore, not permissible,
since existing positions are deleted.

10. Item #10, concerning the correction of a clerical error,
is within §187.2(d)(1) and is, therefore, permissible.

of California'

emonandum

Department of the Youth Authority

Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, California

Date: May 16, 1977

Subject: Request For Approval of
Non-Substantive Alterat
to Our Conflict of
Interest Code

from : Division of Personnel Management

The attached Exhibit A reflects necessary changes to Section 4029,
Enumeration of Designated Positions.

The changes are due to a reorganization. The former Parole and
Institutions Branch has been split to form two branches. The new
names are Institutions & Camps Branch and Parole Services Branch.
This organization change has created some new positions and changed
responsibilities for others.

Two new positions, Deputy Director and Assistant Deputy Director,
Parole Services Branch are designated. The Deputy Director is a
member of the Director's Executive Team, sets policy and is the
administrative head of the Parole Services Branch. The Assistant
Deputy Director has delegated responsibility within the Parole
Services Branch to make policy decisions independently or with
the Deputy Director as well as to participate in the overall
administration of the branch [illegible].

Changes in responsibility of previously designated positions which
have resulted from the organizational change are as follows:

-Deputy Director, Institutions & Camps Branch is no longer responsible for services to parolees, (Category IIC (1)).

-Chiefs, Northern & Southern Divisions, Institutions and Camps
Branch are no longer responsible for services to parolees and are
no longer involved in determining location of parole offices.
(Category I, IIC (1)).

-Parole Regional Administrators (formerly Zones) have more responsibility in determining location of parole offices, therefore should
be required to disclose real estate investments. (Category I)

Other changes include changing the name of all Parole Zones to
Parole Regions and Parole Zone Administrator to Regional Administrators;
some parole offices have closed. There also has been some reassignment
of special projects to parole regions.

Fair Political Practices Commission                                May 16, 1977


Under Karl Holton School, Category II " was inadvertently left off for
the Superintendent and Assistant Superintendent. This is in conformity
with all other institutions disclosure categories for those two positions.

Under Division of Staff Services the following positions have been
eliminated: Chief, Operations Planning Program Monitoring Section;
Supervisor of Correctional Education Program; Community Centered Drug
Administrator.

Other changes in the department include the consolidation of the Division
of Planning and Program Development with the Division of Program Evaluation
to form Division of Planning and Program Analysis. There are no new
positions as a result of this change.

Your early approval of these changes will be appreciated. Our Conflict of
Interest Code will become effective May 20, 1977 with June 20, 1977 as the
final date for filing disclosure statements.


                                                    Sherrie E. Warske
                                                    Staff Services Analyst
                                                    (916) 445-0390

State of California

Memorandum

To : Executive Director
     Fair Political Practices Commission
     1100 "K" Street
     Sacramento, California

Date: May 16, 1977

Subject: Request For Approval of a
          Non-Substantive Alteration
          to Our Conflict of
          Interest Code

From : Division of Personnel Management

The attached Exhibit A reflects necessary changes to Section 4029,
Enumeration of Designated Positions.

The changes are due to a reorganization. The former Parole and
Institutions Branch has been split to form two branches. The new
names are Institutions & Camps Branch and Parole Services Branch.
This organization change has created some new positions and changed
responsibilities for others.

Two new positions, Deputy Director and Assistant Deputy Director,
Parole Services Branch are designated. The Deputy Director is a
member of the Director's Executive Team, sets policy and is the
administrative head of the Parole Services Branch. The Assistant
Deputy Director has delegated responsibility within the Parole
Services Branch to make policy decisions independently or with
the Deputy Director as well as to participate in the overall
administration of the branch itself.

Changes in responsibility of previously designated positions which
have resulted from the organizational change are as follows:

-Deputy Director, Institutions & Camps Branch is no longer respon-
sible for services to parolees. (Category IIC (1)).

-Chiefs, Northern & Southern Divisions, Institutions and Camps
Branch are no longer responsible for services to parolees and are
no longer involved in determining location of parole offices.
(Category I, IIC (1)).

-Parole Regional Administrators (formerly Zones) have more respon-
sibility in determining location of parole offices, therefore should
be required to disclose real estate investments. (Category I)

Other changes include changing the name of all Parole Zones to
Parole Regions and Parole Zone Administrator to Regional Administrators;
some parole offices have closed. There also has been some reassignment
of special projects to parole regions.

-2-
Fair Political Practices Commission                                May 16, 1977


Under Karl Holton School, Category II H was inadvertantly left off for
the Superintendent and Assistant Superintendent. This is in conformity
with all other institutions disclosure categories for those two positions.

Under Division of Staff Services the following positions have been
eliminated: Chief, Operations Planning Program Monitoring Section;
Supervisor of Correctional Education Program; Community Centered Drug
Administrator.

Other changes in the department include the consolidation of the Division
of Planning and Program Development with the Division of Program Evaluation
to form Division of Planning and Program Analysis. There are no new
positions as a result of this change.

Your early approval of these changes will be appreciated. Our Conflict of
Interest Code will become effective May 20, 1977 with June 20, 1977 as the
final date for filing disclosure statements.

Sherrie E. Marske
Staff Services Analyst
(916) 445-0390
